name: Charles Coughlin
description: Canadian-American Catholic priest and radio commentator (1891–1979)
abstract: Charles Edward Coughlin ( kawg-lin; October 25, 1891 – October 27, 1979), commonly known as Father Coughlin, was a Canadian-American Catholic priest based near Detroit. He was the founding priest of the National Shrine of the Little Flower.

about_quotes.speaker: Hentoff Nat
about_quotes.evidence: Father Coughlin is mentioned by the interviewer as part of a rhetorical question about historical context ('...go back then, to Father Coughlin and lynchings...'). The interviewee does not mention Father Coughlin in their response, nor does the text indicate any personal interaction or relationship
about_quotes.doc_title: Hentoff Nat — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
